analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 9 finding(s) from 10 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: No privilege or debug-authentication check before CSR accesses; debug halt alone grants full CSR control.
findings.vulnerability_category: CSR read/write via debug interface / security-critical CSRs (mstatus, mie, etc.) / permission-related RTL security issue

evidence.description: CSR read access: csr_req_n = 1'b1 when debug_halted_o and debug_addr_i[14]
Write access to GPR; similar logic for CSR writes (not fully shown but expected)

findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: zeroriscy_debug_unit Operation: CSR read/write via debug interface Object: security-critical CSRs (mstatus, mie, etc.) Expected guard: debug authentication/lock and/or privileged mode check Observed behavior: Debug unit directly requests CSR access (csr_req_n = 1) whenever debug_halted_o is true, without any authentication or privilege check. Only halt state gates CSR access. Path: debug_req_i -> debug_halted_o -> csr_req_n/we_n Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: An attacker halting the core via debug can read/write all CSRs, bypassing any secure mode restrictions.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Complete debug unit write path for CSRs; possible debug lock/auth signals outside this module not visible.

findings.summary: Cannot assess; file truncated after register address assigns.
findings.vulnerability_category: override interrupt security routing / core_irq_sec_o or interrupt logic / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: test_mode_i Operation: override interrupt security routing Object: core_irq_sec_o or interrupt logic Expected guard: test_mode_i shall not affect security-critical signals in functional mode Observed behavior: test_mode_i is an input port of apb_interrupt_cntrl, but its usage is not visible in the available (truncated) file snippets. Path: test_mode_i -> unknown Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Full implementation of apb_interrupt_cntrl; logic involving test_mode_i.

findings.summary: Interface exists but no security gating visible; configuration-dependent.
findings.vulnerability_category: enable BIST and read bist_result / internal memory/register states / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: BIST control interface Operation: enable BIST and read bist_result Object: internal memory/register states Expected guard: BIST enable locked or tied low in functional mode Observed behavior: BIST_BUS interface defines bist_en, bist_result, etc., with master/slave modports, but no evidence of tie-off or lock logic. Path: bist_en signal from master -> BIST engine Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Potential exposure of internal state if BIST is accessible.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Actual instantiation and connection of BIST; whether bist_en is tied low or controlled by a secure register.

findings.summary: Bypass/flush control lacks security qualification, allowing any bus master to abuse cache operations.
findings.vulnerability_category: assert bypass_req or flush_req / cache lines potentially containing secure data / permission-related RTL security issue

evidence.description: L2_MEM_BUS Master/Slave modports with bypass_req, flush_req, no security attributes

findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: Cache bypass/flush control Operation: assert bypass_req or flush_req Object: cache lines potentially containing secure data Expected guard: bypass/flush requests qualified by security attribute Observed behavior: L2_MEM_BUS interfaces include bypass_req, flush_req, but no security sideband signals (e.g., aw_prot, secure) are present in the interface definition. Path: bypass_req/flush_req from master to cache slave interface without security attribute Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Untrusted masters can flush or bypass cache, potentially leaking secure data or breaking confidentiality.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Internal cache controller logic; integration mapping of bus master security identifiers (if any).

findings.summary: Cannot determine if lock bits exist; need full module.
findings.vulnerability_category: write to interrupt enable/priority registers / interrupt configuration registers / permission-related RTL security issue

evidence.description: Register address map and write decode, truncated before full logic

findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: APB bus master Operation: write to interrupt enable/priority registers Object: interrupt configuration registers Expected guard: lock bits or privilege check Observed behavior: Register addresses defined; writes via APB are decoded but file truncated before register update logic. No lock bit evidence seen in available snippet. Path: APB write -> register update Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Complete register update logic in apb_interrupt_cntrl; existence of lock mechanisms.

findings.summary: Demux lacks security attribute awareness; trust boundary depends solely on external decoder.
findings.vulnerability_category: route memory request based on port_sel_i / secure memory/peripherals accessible via slave ports / permission-related RTL security issue

evidence.description: Entire demux module; no security sideband; purely combinatorial routing based on port_sel_i

findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: fc_demux Operation: route memory request based on port_sel_i Object: secure memory/peripherals accessible via slave ports Expected guard: address range or security attribute check Observed behavior: fc_demux simply routes incoming requests to master_port0 or master_port1 depending on port_sel_i. No security sideband signal (e.g., sec bit) is propagated or checked. Path: slave_port -> (port_sel_i) -> master_port0/1 Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: A non-secure master could drive a transaction destined for a secure region if the external address decoder erroneously selects that port without security attributes.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Implementation of address decoder that drives port_sel_i; whether it considers security attributes.

findings.summary: If the flip-flop version is used, obligation is satisfied; otherwise, it's violated.
findings.vulnerability_category: read register file before explicit write / register file entries / permission-related RTL security issue

evidence.description: FF-based regfile; always_ff with rst_n reset to 0

findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: code executing after reset Operation: read register file before explicit write Object: register file entries Expected guard: full zeroing on reset deassertion Observed behavior: Two register file implementations exist: latch-based (zeroriscy_register_file.sv) with no reset, and flip-flop based (zeroriscy_register_file_ff.sv) with synchronous reset to zero. The flip-flop version satisfies zeroing; latch version does not. Which one is synthesized is unknown. Path: reset sequence to register file Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: If latch version is used, residual data may survive reset, leaking information across security domains.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Configuration parameter or synthesis directive selecting the register file variant.

findings.summary: Core provides no memory protection; secure isolation must be enforced externally.
findings.vulnerability_category: memory read/write to protected address / protected memory region / permission-related RTL security issue

evidence.description: LSU module; no PMP interface or address check

findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: CPU (load/store unit) Operation: memory read/write to protected address Object: protected memory region Expected guard: PMP or memory protection unit gating Observed behavior: LSU sends data_req_o, data_addr_o, etc., directly based on data_req_ex_i from EX stage. No PMP logic is present in the core; no PMP module or address checks are referenced in the zero-riscy subtree. Path: data_req_ex_i -> data_req_o without PMP Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: All memory accesses are unfiltered; any master can access any physical address, breaking secure isolation.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Presence of PMP at SoC level (but core itself has none).

findings.vulnerability_category: assert irq_sec_o based on core_secure_mode_i / secure interrupt output / permission-related RTL security issue

evidence.description: Secure-related ports present but logic truncated

findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: interrupt controller secure routing Operation: assert irq_sec_o based on core_secure_mode_i Object: secure interrupt output Expected guard: correct state machine and configuration Observed behavior: File truncated; port list includes core_irq_sec_o, core_secure_mode_i, and parameter ENA_SEC_IRQ. Implementation not visible. Path: interrupt logic -> core_irq_sec_o Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Full security routing logic and state machine of the interrupt controller.

global_uncertainty: No closure-level missing evidence was recorded beyond the visible source scope limitations.
